acceptance_targets: [chapter_03_prose_01] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 contain no more than one narrator-side 'That was...' or 'It was...' summary opener across those beats, and each moment now lands through specific action, sensation, or social consequence. FAIL if multiple abstract deictic labels remain in those same pivots.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 contain no more than one narrator-side 'That was...' or 'It was...' summary opener across those beats, and each moment now lands through specific action, sensation, or social consequence. FAIL if multiple abstract deictic labels remain in those same pivots.
findings.problem: The narration reuses deictic summary stems often enough to become a tic: 'That was the thing,' 'That was the dangerous part too,' 'It was such a small thing,' 'That was part of the problem,' 'It was too exact.' The surrounding prose is more vivid than these labels; repeated abstract throat-clearing slightly flattens the chapter's strongest emotional turns.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least three of these summary openers at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 with concrete sensory or social detail that lands the beat directly through Lena's body, the room, or June's face. Keep the interiority, but stop announcing the meaning before the image does the work.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
